items.title: Harry Kane fires huge warning to Argentina ahead of England World Cup semi as Lionel Messi braces for ‘special’ battle
items.summary: HARRY KANE vows England WILL improve ahead of Wednesday’s World Cup semi-final showdown with bitter rivals Argentina in Atlanta. And superstar Lionel Messi is relishing a “special” first meeting with  Thomas Tuchel’s “powerhouse” Three Lions. Kane knows England were lucky to seal a 2-1 comeback

items.title: Papers: Arsenal turn to Alvarez with Barcola unavailable
items.summary: The top stories and transfer rumours from Monday's newspapers...

items.title: Arsenal want Argentina's Alvarez - Monday's gossip
items.summary: Arsenal want to sign Atletico Madrid's Julian Alvarez, Aston Villa hijack Newcastle's move for Johan Manzambi, plus more.

items.title: Four things England MUST do to beat Argentina on Wednesday and end Lionel Messi’s hopes of back-to-back World Cup wins
items.summary: ARGENTINA look so beatable. Yet no-one beats them, and England need to work out why. Lionel Scaloni’s side have obvious strengths, starting with their miraculous 39-year-old captain. But Switzerland, like Cape Verde and Egypt before them, exposed their weaknesses. Thomas Tuchel’s job is to exploit t

items.title: England gear up for first ever clash with Lionel Messi in World Cup semi as millions set to flood pubs to watch match
items.summary: ENGLAND were gearing up yesterday for a titanic clash with Argentina for a place in the World Cup final — and a first battle with football colossus Lionel Messi. Incredibly, Messi, 39, has never played against England despite being capped more than 200 times in a glittering 21-year international car

items.title: Infantino: 64-team World Cup will be 'looked at'
items.summary: Gianni Infantino has confirmed FIFA will discuss expanding the men's World Cup to 64 teams after the 2026 tournament, saying every nation should have the chance to dream of playing at football's biggest event.

items.title: Haaland Sr: Norway 'robbed' in QF, 'well done ref'...
items.summary: Alf-Inge Haaland claims Norway were "robbed" in their World Cup quarterfinal but hopes England go on to win the tournament after knocking out his homeland.

items.title: A-List: FIFA's top-4 make WC semis for first time
items.summary: Argentina, Spain, France and England went into the tournament as the top four in FIFA's rankings and are two wins away from being crowned champions again.

items.title: Messi praises 'not normal' Argentina in WC defense...
items.summary: Lionel Messi has said Argentina's remarkable run of success is "not normal" after the reigning world champions beat Switzerland to reach the World Cup semifinals, where they will face England.

items.title: Sources: FIFA, FIFPRO in talks over heat protocol ...
items.summary: FIFA and global players' union FIFPRO are in discussions over establishing protocols to avoid games being played in excessive heat, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Norway boss fumes at 'clear' TV wire goal assist
items.summary: Norway boss Stale Solbakken has said he believes the overhead wires deserve an assist for England's equaliser.

items.title: Copy of Embolo red-carded for diving after VAR review
items.summary: Switzerland forward Breel Embolo was sent off in the second half of a World Cup quarterfinal match with Argentina on Saturday night after a video review showed he was guilty of simulation.

items.title: Bellingham backs England stars after Tuchel fury
items.summary: Jude Bellingham praised England's fighting spirit and ability to "win dirty" after coach Thomas Tuchel criticized their performance in beating Norway on Saturday.

items.title: ⚽ 'Ted Lasso' actor Fernández debuts in USL
items.summary: Fernández, a forward like Rojas, was subbed on in the 79th minute of El Paso's USL Cup match against New Mexico United on Saturday night.
